text.title: aws-iam-practical-patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for aws-iam-practical-patterns. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: IAM policies for application workloads MUST scope every Action to the minimum set required and every Resource to the specific ARN or ARN prefix (not "*"). Add Condition blocks to restrict to specific resource tags (aws:ResourceTag) or account paths. Never allow Action: "*" or Resource: "*" in application policies.
rule.rationale: Wildcard actions on wildcard resources grant an attacker full account access if the credential is compromised. Resource-level constraints limit the blast radius to only the resources the service legitimately touches.

rule.statement: Every EC2 instance MUST have an IAM instance role (not access keys) that includes at minimum AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore. Attach CloudWatchAgentServerPolicy for metrics/logs. Add application-specific inline policies scoped to the resources the instance actually needs. Create an instance profile and associate it with the role before attaching to an instance.
rule.rationale: Instance roles provide auto-rotating credentials via IMDS. Access keys embedded in instances never rotate automatically and survive AMI copies. SSM Core policy enables Session Manager, Patch Manager, and Run Command without SSH.

rule.statement: Cross-account AssumeRole trust policies MUST require MFA (aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent: true) for human access and MUST include a unique ExternalId condition for third-party tool access. The source account principal MUST be scoped to a specific role ARN or account, never "arn:aws:iam::*:root".
rule.rationale: Without MFA enforcement, a compromised long-term credential in the source account can assume cross-account roles. ExternalId prevents the confused deputy problem where a third-party SaaS tool is tricked into accessing the wrong customer account.

rule.statement: When delegating IAM role creation to a team, attach a permission boundary to every role they create. The boundary MUST explicitly Deny iam:CreateUser, iam:DeleteUser, iam:CreateRole, iam:DeleteRole, iam:AttachRolePolicy, iam:DetachRolePolicy, iam:PutRolePermissionsBoundary, iam:DeleteRolePermissionsBoundary, and all organizations:* actions. The delegated admin SCP or IAM policy MUST require iam:PermissionsBoundary on role creation.
rule.rationale: A delegated admin without a permission boundary can create a role with AdministratorAccess and assume it, escaping all delegation constraints. The boundary is the only hard limit on effective permissions.
